# UC-13.6.8 · Grafana API Token Usage and Expiration Monitoring

## Description

Expired Grafana tokens break automation silently until dashboards or exporters fail en masse. Tracking creation, revocation, and time-to-live centrally prevents surprise outages in CI and observability glue code.

## Value

Reduces authentication-related incident volume and supports key rotation compliance.

## Implementation

If `expires_at` is absent, join to `/api/auth/keys` nightly snapshot. Never log raw secret; use token fingerprint. Alert when `ttl_days<7` for tokens used by production data pipelines.

## SPL

```spl
index=observability sourcetype="grafana:audit"
| search token OR "service account"
| eval expires_ts=strptime(expires_at,"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ")
| eval days_left=round((expires_ts-now())/86400,1)
| where action="create" OR action="revoke" OR days_left < 14
| stats latest(days_left) as ttl_days, latest(action) as last_action, values(name) as token_name by token_id, user
| sort ttl_days
```

## Visualization

Gantt-style TTL chart, table (token_name × ttl_days), pie of tokens by owner team.

## References

- [Grafana — HTTP API authentication](https://grafana.com/docs/grafana/latest/developers/http_api/authentication/)
